label: Present a credential
description: Prove something to a service, organisation, or authority by presenting an existing credential with clear consent and a reliable outcome.
use_when: A service needs proof of identity, status, eligibility, or entitlement.
The service currently requests documents or certificates.
The service wants to reduce repeated document requests and revalidation.
do_not_use_when: No credential exists — the user must obtain one first.

building_blocks.notes: Required before sharing credential data with the verifier.

steps.label: Understand the proof request
steps.description: Explain what is being proven, why it is needed, and what happens after proof is accepted or rejected.

steps.label: Choose how to proceed
steps.description: User selects route: digital wallet, paper/assisted, or on-site reference lookup.

steps.label: Select credential from wallet
steps.description: User selects which credential to present.

steps.label: Enter reference and match record
steps.description: User enters a reference number to retrieve their credential on-site.

steps.label: Present proof
steps.description: Proof is presented via chosen route: wallet, QR, reference, paper, or assisted verification.

steps.label: See outcome and return to service

ux_considerations.text: User consent is essential. Presenting proof must feel like an informed choice, not a hidden data transfer.
Offer paper or assisted routes where digital is not possible or appropriate.
If presentation fails, do not leave users at a dead end — enable retry, alternative proof, or assisted support.

technical_notes.text: Support offline credential presentation where feasible — avoid hard connectivity dependency.
